title: PIM Integration — Work Breakdown Structure
agenda_items: PIM API exploration via APG portal
Product linkage approach between Picasso and PIM

discussion_points.title: PIM Overview
discussion_points.description: PIM is the legacy product storage system. Access is read-only and requires completing self-learning materials and passing a quiz.
details: PIM stores legacy products and supports advanced search by product attributes
Many attributes exist but their meaning is not yet documented or understood
Access is read-only — no QA/dev environment available
Access is granted automatically after completing self-learning and passing a quiz
Iryna shared two links in chat: PIM access materials and the learning path with the quiz

discussion_points.title: Integration Goals
discussion_points.description: Two main goals: understand how PIM structure maps to Picasso, and manage historical/legacy product data across teams that create products differently.
details: Map PIM product structure to Picasso product model
Handle historical data — different teams use different product creation approaches
Integration is outbound only (Picasso reads from PIM, no write-back)

discussion_points.title: PIM APIs via APG Portal
discussion_points.description: PIM exposes approximately 10 APIs via the APG portal, covering product data, contribution, document files, incremental updates, and search.
details: APIs available: product, contribution, document file, incremental, search
Product API has multiple endpoints; search has only one endpoint
API documentation is available on the APG portal (accessible via VPN)
VDI and regular VPN use different accounts — may affect portal access
Exact API to use is not yet decided; analysis required

discussion_points.title: Product Linkage Approach
discussion_points.description: Picasso products can optionally be linked to a corresponding PIM record via a unique ID, but the link is not mandatory.
details: Products are registered in Picasso by users first, then can be linked to a PIM record
Some Picasso products will never have a PIM record (internal applications, pre-commercialized products)
Products in development not yet released to market will not appear in PIM until they get a commercial reference number
Product creation in Picasso without a PIM link must remain supported
UI needed: search for PIM product and UI to establish the link

discussion_points.title: Attribute Synchronization
discussion_points.description: Once a Picasso product is linked to PIM, a small set of attributes (~2–5) will be monitored and synced from PIM into Picasso.
details: Product lifecycle state is the primary attribute to sync (active, end of commercialization, end of support, end of life)
Attribute mapping between PIM values and Picasso values will be configured in back office
Example: PIM may use 'duplicated' where Picasso uses 'end of life' — mapping needed
Exact set of attributes to sync is not yet identified; further analysis required

key_decisions: Integration will be read-only (outbound) — Picasso consumes PIM data, no write-back to PIM
PIM APIs will be accessed via the APG portal
PIM linkage for a Picasso product is optional — products can exist without a PIM record
Status/attribute value mapping between PIM and Picasso will be handled in back office configuration

action_items.item: Request PIM read-only access, complete self-learning materials, and pass the quiz
Share PIM documentation links and screenshots with Ramesh
Share PIM and legacy product screenshots in the team chat

open_items: Which specific PIM API will be used (PIM Core, PIM Search, or other) — depends on analysis
Exact set of product attributes to synchronize from PIM to Picasso
Exact data mapping between PIM attribute values and Picasso values
Point of contact at the PIM team for API credentials and integration support (Hussain, Hiba, and Rahul Sharma mentioned but no confirmed contact established)
Whether two-way communication channel with PIM team representatives is needed
next_steps: Ramesh to gain PIM access and explore APIs on APG portal
Team to regroup later this week or next week once Ramesh has explored PIM and APIs
Produce high-level estimates for each WBS topic based on updated knowledge
next_meeting: Later this week or next week (exact date not scheduled)
summary: The team held a kickoff session to scope the PIM integration into Picasso. The integration will be read-only — Picasso will consume product data from PIM via APG portal APIs to synchronize a small set of attributes (primarily product lifecycle state). Linking a Picasso product to a PIM record will be optional, as some products (internal apps, pre-commercialized items) will never exist in PIM. Key next steps are for Ramesh to obtain PIM access and explore the available APIs, after which the team will regroup to produce high-level effort estimates.
